1.title: The feast at Shushan
1.text: After the hundred and eighty days, the king holds a further seven-day feast for all the people in the court of the garden of the royal palace at Shushan.

1.question: Where did the king hold the seven-day feast?
options: In the court of the garden of the royal palace, at Shushan
In a tent in the wilderness

1.question: Who made a feast for the women in the royal house?
How many princes of Persia and Media saw the king's face?
According to the ladies, Vashti's deed would bring among the women
What did the king decree by letters to all the provinces?
options: That every man should bear rule in his own house
That Vashti return to the throne

2.title: Seeking a new queen
2.text: With the king's wrath appeased, his servants propose gathering fair young virgins; the one who pleased the king would reign in Vashti's place.

2.question: Who would reign in Vashti's place?
options: The maiden who pleased the king

2.question: How did Hegai, keeper of the women, treat Esther?
options: She obtained kindness and was preferred to the best place of the house of the women
She was rejected and cast out
She was put among the concubines

2.question: What happened to the two chamberlains who plotted against the king?
options: They were both hanged on a tree

3.title: Haman's wrath
3.text: Haman, exalted by the king, is full of wrath because Mordecai will not bow; he resolves to destroy not only Mordecai but all the Jews in the kingdom.

3.question: What did Haman seek to do when he learned Mordecai was a Jew?
options: Destroy all the Jews in the kingdom of Ahasuerus

3.question: What did the king give Haman when he agreed to his plan?
How was the city of Shushan when the decree was proclaimed?

4.title: Mordecai's mourning
4.text: Learning of the decree, Mordecai puts on sackcloth and ashes and cries through the city; Esther, on hearing of it, is exceedingly grieved.

4.question: How did Mordecai react when he learned of the decree?
options: He put on sackcloth and ashes and cried through the city
He went to speak with Haman

4.question: Who brought Mordecai's words to Esther?
What did Mordecai warn Esther about her own danger?
options: That she would not escape in the king's house more than the other Jews
That she must flee the palace
That she must reveal all to Haman

4.question: What did Mordecai do after Esther's answer?
options: He did according to all that Esther had commanded him

5.title: Esther's first banquet
5.text: The king receives Esther and, eager for her request, calls for Haman to make haste and come to the banquet she had prepared.

5.question: Whom did Esther invite to her banquet along with the king?
Whom did Haman call for when he came home?
options: His friends and Zeresh his wife

5.question: What did Zeresh and his friends advise Haman to do?
options: Have a gallows made to hang Mordecai

6.title: The sleepless night
6.text: Unable to sleep, the king has the chronicles read and discovers that Mordecai, who had exposed the plot, had never been honored.

6.question: Who entered the court just as the king sought how to honor Mordecai?
To whom did the king command Haman to render the honor he had described?
Where did the chamberlains hasten Haman at the chapter's end?
options: To the banquet Esther had prepared

7.title: Esther reveals her people
7.text: At the second banquet, Esther asks the king for her own life and that of her people, declaring they have been sold to be destroyed.

7.question: What did Esther declare had happened to her and her people?
options: They were sold to be destroyed, slain, and to perish
They were exalted by the king

7.question: What happened to Haman at the end of the chapter?
options: He was hanged on the gallows he had prepared for Mordecai
He was pardoned by the king

8.title: Esther's plea
8.text: Esther falls at the king's feet and begs him to reverse Haman's device, for she cannot endure to see the evil that shall come to her people.

8.question: Why was Esther pleading with the king?
options: She could not endure to see the evil that shall come to her people
She wanted to leave the palace

8.question: On what day could the Jews defend themselves, by the new decree?
options: On the thirteenth day of the twelfth month, the month Adar
On the first day of the year

8.question: What did many of the people of the land do because of the fear of the Jews?

9.title: Mordecai's greatness
9.text: On the day the enemies hoped to overpower the Jews, the opposite came to pass; and Mordecai waxed greater and greater in the king's house.

9.question: How was Mordecai in the king's house?
options: Great, and his fame went throughout all the provinces

9.question: What did the Jews NOT do after slaying Haman's ten sons?
options: They laid not their hand on the spoil

9.question: How many men did the Jews of Shushan slay on the fourteenth of Adar?
What did the Jews of the villages make of the fourteenth of Adar?
options: A day of gladness, feasting, and sending portions one to another
A day of mourning and fasting

9.question: What happened to Haman's wicked device, according to the king's letters?
options: It returned upon his own head, and he and his sons were hanged
It was carried out against the Jews

9.question: What confirmed the matters of that Purim and was written in the book?

10.title: Mordecai, second in the kingdom
10.text: Mordecai becomes second only to king Ahasuerus, great among the Jews, seeking the good of his people and speaking peace for all his nation.

10.question: What was Mordecai's position at the end of the book?
options: Next unto king Ahasuerus, and great among the Jews
